QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_44

question.text: Ankit contributes Rs.2 lakh per year to NPS Tier I. His employer contributes Rs.1.5 lakh. After 5 years with total returns of Rs.1 lakh, what is his maximum partial withdrawal amount?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Partial withdrawal is 25% of subscriber's OWN contributions only. Ankit's own contributions = Rs.2 lakh x 5 = Rs.10 lakh. 25% of Rs.10 lakh = Rs.2.5 lakh. **The Trap:** The trap is including employer contributions (Rs.1.5 lakh x 5 = Rs.7.5 lakh) or returns (Rs.1 lakh) in the base. Only own contributions count. **Cross-Exam Context:** Own contributions only: Rs.10 lakh. Not total corpus (Rs.10L + Rs.7.5L + Rs.1L = Rs.18.5 lakh). 25% of Rs.10L = Rs.2.5 lakh. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always calculate partial withdrawal eligibility based on own contributions only. Show clients the difference between their total corpus and the withdrawal base.
